name: Mexico
qualification: Qualified automatically as co-host of the 2026 FIFA World Cup alongside the USA and Canada. Mexico did not need to go through CONCACAF qualifying.
style: Possession-based with quick passing combinations. Mexico traditionally plays a technically skilled, short-passing game that relies on midfield control. They can struggle against physical, direct teams.

offense.explanation: Strong attacking options with players like Santiago Giménez and Hirving Lozano. Creative midfield can unlock defenses with intricate passing.

defense.explanation: Defensively improved under Aguirre but still prone to lapses against top opposition. Center-back depth remains a concern.

form: Mixed club form in 2025-26. Santiago Giménez has been prolific at Feyenoord, but several key players have had inconsistent seasons at their European clubs. The team's preparation as hosts has focused on friendlies with mixed results.
world_cup_history: Mexico has appeared in 17 World Cups with a best finish of quarterfinals in 1970 and 1986 (both as hosts). They have famously been eliminated in the Round of 16 in the last eight consecutive tournaments - the dreaded 'quinto partido' curse.
interesting_fact: Mexico is desperate to break the Round of 16 curse that has haunted them since 1986. As co-hosts playing in front of passionate home crowds, this is seen as their best-ever chance to finally reach the quarterfinals. The pressure on Aguirre and the squad is immense - failure at a home World Cup would be a national catastrophe.
prediction_notes: As co-hosts, Mexico will have enormous crowd support and should advance from the group stage. Their ceiling depends on whether they can finally overcome the Round of 16 barrier. A quarterfinal appearance would be considered a success.

name: South Africa
qualification: Qualified through CAF qualifying, finishing in the top qualifying positions in their group. Bafana Bafana rode a wave of momentum following their impressive 2023 AFCON third-place finish to secure their spot.
style: Defensively disciplined with quick counter-attacks. Under Broos, South Africa plays a compact defensive shape and looks to exploit transitions with pace on the wings.

offense.explanation: Limited in creating chances against organized defenses. Rely heavily on individual brilliance from key attackers and set pieces.

defense.explanation: Solid defensive organization under Broos. Well-drilled backline that is difficult to break down, though individual quality can be exposed by top teams.

form: Mamelodi Sundowns players have been dominant in the South African league, providing a strong core. Percy Tau has performed well in the CAF Champions League with Al Ahly. The squad is well-drilled and confident after good qualifying form.
world_cup_history: South Africa hosted the 2010 World Cup but were eliminated in the group stage - the first and only host nation to fail to advance. They also participated in 1998 and 2002. This is their first World Cup appearance since hosting in 2010.
interesting_fact: South Africa returns to the World Cup for the first time since they hosted in 2010, where they became the only host nation ever eliminated in the group stage. The pain of that exit still haunts the nation. This tournament represents redemption - a chance to prove that South African football has grown and can compete on the world stage on its own merits, not just as hosts.
prediction_notes: South Africa are underdogs in Group A but should not be underestimated. Their defensive organization and AFCON experience give them a chance to cause upsets. Advancing from the group would be a major achievement.

name: South Korea
qualification: Qualified through AFC qualifying, securing their spot in the third round of Asian qualifiers. South Korea continued their remarkable streak of qualifying for every World Cup since 1986.
style: High-energy, pressing-based football with quick transitions. South Korea plays with incredible work rate and intensity, pressing opponents high and using pace in attack. They adapt well between possession and counter-attacking styles.

offense.explanation: Son Heung-min remains world-class and leads a dynamic attack. Supporting cast including Lee Kang-in provides creativity and goal threat from multiple positions.

defense.explanation: Organized but can be exposed by top-quality attackers. Kim Min-jae anchors the defense but the full-back positions can be vulnerable.

form: Son Heung-min has had another strong season at Tottenham, while Kim Min-jae has been solid at Bayern Munich. Lee Kang-in has grown into an important player at PSG. The European-based core brings top-level experience.
world_cup_history: South Korea's greatest moment was reaching the semifinals in 2002 as co-hosts, finishing fourth. They have qualified for every World Cup since 1986 (11 consecutive). They reached the Round of 16 in 2010 and had a famous victory over Germany in 2018.
interesting_fact: Son Heung-min, now 33, is almost certainly playing in his last World Cup. Asia's greatest-ever footballer wants to cap his career with a deep run. After controversially missing the 2022 World Cup group stage opener due to a facial injury, Son is determined to lead his nation to glory one final time.
prediction_notes: South Korea are a solid dark horse in Group A with genuine quality in Son, Kim, and Lee. They should be favorites to advance from the group. Their pressing intensity could trouble any opponent, but consistency over seven games remains a question.

interesting_fact: Czechia qualified for the World Cup for only the second time as an independent nation (first since 2006). The country officially rebranded from 'Czech Republic' to 'Czechia' and this tournament feels like a fresh start for a nation rebuilding its football identity after years of disappointing campaigns.
prediction_notes: Czechia are a tough, organized side that will be hard to beat but may lack the firepower to dominate. They could be the group's spoilers. A Round of 16 appearance would be a good result.

Alphonso Davies moved to Real Madrid, Jonathan David joined Barcelona - this is a team with players at the biggest clubs in the world. For a country that went 36 years between World Cup appearances, hosting the tournament feels like a fairy tale. The entire nation is dreaming of something historic.
prediction_notes: Canada are dark horses with genuine star power in Davies and David. Home advantage and the momentum of their golden generation give them a real chance to make a deep run. They should advance from the group comfortably.

His potential farewell adds emotional weight to every match.

interesting_fact: Brazil's 24-year World Cup drought is their longest ever, and it haunts the football-mad nation. The 7-1 humiliation at home in 2014 and the disappointing 2022 quarterfinal exit have created a crisis of confidence. Vinicius Jr. carries the weight of expectation to restore Brazil to glory - he is the new face of Brazilian football, but also faces racist abuse that has become a global talking point.
prediction_notes: Brazil are among the favorites with extraordinary attacking talent, particularly Vinicius Jr. Their main concerns are defensive consistency and the psychological pressure of ending their long drought. If they find the right balance, they have the quality to win it all.

They play with the chip-on-the-shoulder energy of proving doubters wrong.
prediction_notes: Morocco are genuine contenders and no longer a surprise package. Their defensive excellence and counter-attacking quality make them dangerous against anyone. They should advance from the group and could go deep again - a quarterfinal at minimum is expected.

The team carries the flag for a nation that has been through unimaginable hardship.

qualification: Qualified through UEFA qualifying, securing their place via the Nations League pathway. Scotland earned their first World Cup berth since 1998 in a dramatic qualifying campaign.
style: Defensive solidity with direct attacking play. Scotland plays a pragmatic, organized style that prioritizes defensive shape. They use set pieces, crosses, and direct balls to target man John McGinn and the striker. Aggressive pressing in midfield.

offense.explanation: Rely heavily on set pieces and crossing rather than intricate play. Individual quality from players like McGinn can produce goals, but creativity is limited.

defense.explanation: Well-organized under Clarke and difficult to break down when they sit deep. The back three/five system gives them defensive stability.

form: Scott McTominay has been revelatory at Napoli, becoming one of Serie A's most impactful midfielders. Robertson remains elite at Liverpool. Gilmour has settled well in Italy. The squad has genuine quality in key positions.
world_cup_history: Scotland have appeared in 8 World Cups but have never progressed past the group stage - a record that is both remarkable and painful. Their last World Cup was in 1998 in France. They are forever the 'nearly men' of world football.
interesting_fact: Scotland's World Cup drought stretching back to 1998 is finally over, but the even bigger monkey on their back remains: they have NEVER progressed past the World Cup group stage in 8 attempts. The Tartan Army is one of football's most passionate traveling fanbases and their emotional journey to 2026 will be one of the tournament's great stories. Can they finally break the curse?
prediction_notes: Scotland are a solid, organized side that will be competitive in Group C. Advancing past Brazil and Morocco will be extremely difficult, but the expanded format gives them hope. Their midfield quality, especially McTominay, gives them a fighting chance to finally make the knockout rounds.

name: USA
qualification: Qualified automatically as co-host of the 2026 FIFA World Cup alongside Canada and Mexico. The US was awarded hosting duties alongside their North American partners.
style: High-pressing, athletic, and attack-minded under Pochettino. The USMNT plays with intensity and directness, using the pace and athleticism of their young talent. They transition quickly and press aggressively to win the ball back.

offense.explanation: Christian Pulisic, Gio Reyna, and a deep pool of attackers provide excellent offensive weapons. The youth movement means pace, creativity, and unpredictability in the final third.

defense.explanation: The defense has been the question mark. While individually talented, the backline has been prone to errors and lacks the cohesion of the attack. Center-back partnerships remain unsettled.

form: Pulisic has been outstanding at AC Milan, establishing himself as one of Serie A's best players. McKennie is a regular starter at Juventus. The core of the team plays at top European clubs and has gained significant big-game experience.
world_cup_history: The USA reached the semifinals in the inaugural 1930 World Cup and the quarterfinals in 2002. They famously beat England in 1950. After missing the 2018 World Cup, they returned in 2022 and reached the Round of 16. Hosting in 1994 sparked the growth of American soccer.
interesting_fact: This is the moment American soccer has been building toward for 30 years since hosting in 1994. Pochettino was hired to professionalize the operation, and the golden generation of Pulisic, Reyna, McKennie, and Adams are entering their prime. With home advantage, massive crowds, and a point to prove, the pressure to deliver a deep run is unlike anything US Soccer has ever faced. Failure would set the sport back in America; success could transform it forever.
prediction_notes: The USA are among the favorites thanks to home advantage and a talented squad. They have the quality to reach the quarterfinals or beyond. The key will be handling the immense pressure and whether the defense can hold up against elite attackers.

name: Paraguay
qualification: Qualified through CONMEBOL South American qualifying. Paraguay secured one of the continent's spots in a competitive Eliminatorias campaign, returning to the World Cup after missing 2018 and 2022.
style: Gritty, physical, and defensively resilient. Paraguay's football identity is built on never-say-die spirit, tough tackling, and defensive organization. They are difficult to beat and can grind out results against superior opponents.

offense.explanation: Not prolific scorers but capable of finding goals through set pieces and counter-attacks. Individual quality from Miguel Almirón provides spark.

defense.explanation: Paraguay's greatest strength. They are organized, physical, and rarely give up easy goals. The South American qualifying gauntlet has hardened their defensive resolve.

form: Julio Enciso has emerged as an exciting young talent at Brighton, bringing flair to a traditionally gritty squad. Almirón's experience at Newcastle is valuable. Gómez remains a rock at the back with Palmeiras.
world_cup_history: Paraguay have appeared in 8 World Cups with a best finish of quarterfinals in 2010 (South Africa). They also reached the Round of 16 in 1998 and 2002. They missed the last two World Cups in 2018 and 2022.
interesting_fact: Paraguay's return to the World Cup after eight years in the wilderness represents a rebuilding of national football pride. The country has suffered economic hardship and their football federation faced corruption scandals. Young talent like Julio Enciso represents a new generation carrying the hopes of a football-crazy nation desperate to relive the glory days of 2010.
prediction_notes: Paraguay are tough, organized underdogs who will make life difficult for anyone in Group D. Their South American qualifying experience means they are battle-hardened. They could sneak through to the knockout rounds if they grind out results.

name: Australia
qualification: Qualified through AFC qualifying, securing their spot in the Asian third round. The Socceroos continued their recent streak of World Cup appearances with a strong qualifying campaign.
style: Direct, physical, and increasingly technical. Australia combines traditional British-style directness with growing technical quality from their European-based players. They press well and use wide players to deliver crosses.

offense.explanation: Improving with the emergence of young attackers. The team can create chances but sometimes lacks the killer instinct in front of goal.

defense.explanation: Solid and well-organized. Harry Souttar provides aerial dominance and the defensive shape is disciplined. Can be caught by quick, technical teams.

form: Nestory Irankunda has been the breakthrough story, earning a move to Bayern Munich as one of the world's most exciting teenage talents. The squad mixes experienced campaigners with exciting youth. European-based players are performing well at their clubs.
world_cup_history: Australia have appeared in 6 World Cups with a best finish of Round of 16 in 2006. Their most memorable moment was Tim Cahill's goals in 2014. They have qualified for every World Cup since 2006.
interesting_fact: Nestory Irankunda, the Eritrean-born Australian teenager at Bayern Munich, represents the changing face of Australian football. The Socceroos are no longer just an Anglo team - they are a multicultural squad reflecting modern Australia. This could be the tournament where Australian football finally produces a genuine global star.
prediction_notes: Australia are competitive underdogs in Group D who could surprise. Their mix of experience and emerging young talent makes them dangerous. Advancing from a group with the USA and Turkey will be challenging but not impossible.

name: Turkey
qualification: Qualified through UEFA qualifying with a strong campaign under Montella. Turkey topped their qualifying group, showing the kind of consistency that had been lacking in previous cycles.
style: Aggressive, attacking football with flair. Under Montella, Turkey plays an exciting, front-foot style with emphasis on quick passing and pressing. The team has young, dynamic attackers who play with freedom and creativity.

offense.explanation: Arda Güler and Kenan Yıldız lead a thrilling young attack. Turkey's offensive talent is among the most exciting of any non-traditional powerhouse.

defense.explanation: Improved under Montella but still prone to individual errors. The defensive line can be caught out when they overcommit in attack.

form: Arda Güler has been increasingly important at Real Madrid, showing flashes of genius. Kenan Yıldız is a regular at Juventus. Çalhanoğlu remains world-class at Inter. The young core is developing rapidly at the biggest clubs in Europe.
world_cup_history: Turkey's greatest World Cup moment was their stunning third-place finish in 2002 in South Korea/Japan. They also appeared in 1954. They missed every World Cup from 2006 to 2022, making this a long-awaited return.
interesting_fact: Turkey's golden generation of Güler, Yıldız, and other young stars are being hailed as the most talented Turkish squad since the 2002 heroes. Arda Güler, still only 21, is already drawing comparisons to the great Hakan Şükür. The entire nation believes this team can recreate the magic of 2002, and the passionate Turkish fanbase will create an incredible atmosphere at their matches.
prediction_notes: Turkey are one of the most exciting dark horses in the tournament. Their young attacking talent, led by Güler and Yıldız, can trouble any defense. If they can maintain defensive discipline, they have the quality for a deep run. Group D is tough but Turkey have the talent to advance.

name: Germany
qualification: Qualified through UEFA qualifying, topping their group convincingly. Germany's resurgence under Nagelsmann following their Euro 2024 hosting produced impressive qualifying results.
style: High-pressing, possession-based football with tactical flexibility. Under Nagelsmann, Germany combines intense pressing with quick vertical passing. They dominate possession but are also capable of direct, incisive counter-attacks.

offense.explanation: Jamal Musiala and Florian Wirtz form one of the most exciting attacking duos in world football. Kai Havertz provides a goal threat, and the depth of attacking options is excellent.

defense.explanation: Antonio Rüdiger anchors the defense and Jonathan Tah provides solidity. Nagelsmann has restored defensive discipline after years of fragility.

form: Musiala and Wirtz have been sensational in 2025-26, both competing for the Ballon d'Or. Havertz has been excellent at Arsenal. Kimmich remains one of the best midfielders in the world. The squad is peaking at the right time.
world_cup_history: Germany have won 4 World Cups (1954, 1974, 1990, 2014) and been runners-up 4 times. They are the most consistent World Cup nation alongside Brazil. However, they suffered shock group-stage exits in 2018 and 2022.
interesting_fact: Germany's consecutive group-stage exits in 2018 and 2022 were unprecedented humiliations for the four-time champions. Nagelsmann's appointment and the emergence of Musiala and Wirtz have transformed the mood from despair to genuine belief. The Euro 2024 semifinal heartbreak on home soil added fuel to their motivation. This is a team on a redemption mission with generational talent.
prediction_notes: Germany are among the top favorites with arguably the best young attacking talent in the tournament in Musiala and Wirtz. Their group is manageable and they should cruise through. The question is whether they can avoid the knockout-stage collapses that have haunted them recently.

name: Curaçao
qualification: Qualified through CONCACAF qualifying in a historic achievement. The tiny Caribbean island nation earned one of the confederation's expanded World Cup berths, reaching the tournament for the first time in their history.
style: Organized and disciplined with a focus on defensive structure. Curaçao plays a compact defensive game and looks to hit on the counter. Many players have dual nationality with the Netherlands and bring Dutch technical training.

offense.explanation: Limited in creating chances consistently but have individual players with Dutch league or lower European league experience who can provide moments of quality.

defense.explanation: Well-organized under experienced coaching but will be severely tested against top-quality opposition. The defensive structure compensates for individual limitations.

form: Players are scattered across Dutch, English lower division, and MLS clubs. Individual form varies, but the team's strength comes from collective organization rather than individual star power. Their qualification run showed impressive team spirit.
world_cup_history: This is Curaçao's first-ever World Cup appearance. The island, a constituent country of the Netherlands with a population of around 150,000, has been a FIFA member since 1932 (formerly as the Netherlands Antilles until 2010).
interesting_fact: Curaçao is the smallest nation by population (~150,000) to qualify for the 2026 World Cup. Many of their players were born and raised in the Netherlands but chose to represent the island of their heritage. The qualification was celebrated with island-wide parties. For a Caribbean island known more for tourism and colorful architecture, reaching the World Cup is the greatest sporting achievement in their history.
prediction_notes: Curaçao are among the weakest teams in the tournament and will likely be eliminated in the group stage. However, their Dutch coaching and technically sound players could produce an upset moment. Simply being there is already a victory for this tiny nation.

name: Ivory Coast
qualification: Qualified through CAF qualifying following their triumphant 2023 AFCON victory on home soil. The Elephants rode the momentum of that title to secure their World Cup place.
style: Physical, powerful, and technically gifted. Ivory Coast combines African athleticism with technical quality from their European-based players. They play an exciting, attacking style with strength in wide areas.

offense.explanation: Sébastien Haller's return and Nicolas Pépé's experience provide a strong attacking base. The team has pace and power going forward.

defense.explanation: Solid but not elite. The defense can be physical and imposing but may struggle against top-tier attackers who can exploit pace or technical gaps.

form: Simon Adingra has been excellent at Brighton, emerging as one of the Premier League's most exciting wingers. Kossounou is solid at Leverkusen. Haller has battled back from cancer to reclaim his place, an inspirational story. The squad has quality across positions.
world_cup_history: Ivory Coast have appeared in 3 World Cups (2006, 2010, 2014) but have never progressed past the group stage. The golden generation of Drogba, Touré, and Gervinho was famously unlucky with tough group draws.
interesting_fact: Sébastien Haller's journey from testicular cancer diagnosis in 2022 to leading Ivory Coast's attack at the World Cup is one of the most inspirational stories in football. He was told he might never play again, yet he recovered, won the AFCON in 2023, and now leads the line at a World Cup. His presence alone makes Ivory Coast a team to root for.
prediction_notes: Ivory Coast are solid dark horses with AFCON-winning pedigree and exciting attackers. They could trouble any team in Group E. Advancing past the group stage for the first time would be historic and is within reach with the expanded format.

name: Ecuador
qualification: Qualified through CONMEBOL South American qualifying. Ecuador secured their spot in the competitive Eliminatorias, continuing their emergence as a consistent South American qualifier.
style: High-altitude conditioning and intense pressing. Ecuador uses the altitude advantage of Quito in home games and plays an aggressive, high-energy pressing style. They are organized, physical, and dangerous on set pieces.

offense.explanation: Moisés Caicedo drives the team from midfield. The attack has young, exciting talents but lacks a proven world-class striker to convert chances consistently.

defense.explanation: Solid and well-organized, particularly in the center. The defense is the foundation of their play, though they can be vulnerable to pace on the wings.

form: Moisés Caicedo has been one of the Premier League's best midfielders at Chelsea. Pacho has established himself at PSG. Hincapié is a regular at Leverkusen. Ecuador's young core plays at elite European clubs.
world_cup_history: Ecuador have appeared in 3 World Cups (2002, 2006, 2014). Their best finish was the Round of 16 in 2006. They are an emerging South American football nation that has grown significantly since the turn of the century.
interesting_fact: Ecuador's youth revolution is remarkable - Caicedo, Pacho, and Hincapié are all still in their early twenties and playing at the highest level in Europe. This is the most talented squad in Ecuadorian history, and they are determined to prove that South American football isn't just about Brazil, Argentina, and Uruguay. The altitude of Quito has always been their secret weapon in qualifying, but now they must prove themselves at sea level.
prediction_notes: Ecuador are dark horses with impressive defensive talent and Caicedo pulling strings in midfield. They can compete with anyone on their day. Advancing from Group E past Germany is tough, but second place is very achievable.

name: Netherlands
qualification: Qualified through UEFA qualifying, finishing top of their group. The Netherlands continued their return to major tournament prominence under Koeman.
style: Classic Dutch 4-3-3 with modern adaptations. The Netherlands plays possession-based football with emphasis on wide play, pressing, and fluid attacking movements. They combine traditional Total Football principles with pragmatic defensive organization.

offense.explanation: Cody Gakpo has emerged as a world-class attacker, supported by quality from all positions. The Dutch attack is versatile, creative, and clinical.

defense.explanation: Virgil van Dijk remains one of the world's best center-backs despite his age. The defensive unit is solid when organized but can be caught in transition.

form: Gakpo has been exceptional at Liverpool, becoming one of the Premier League's top scorers. Van Dijk continues to be dominant. Xavi Simons has been brilliant at Leipzig. The squad has quality throughout and players are in strong form at top clubs.
world_cup_history: The Netherlands have been runners-up three times (1974, 1978, 2010) and finished third in 2014. Despite being one of football's greatest nations, they have never won the World Cup - a source of eternal frustration. They also failed to qualify in 2002 and 2018.
interesting_fact: The Netherlands' curse of never winning the World Cup despite reaching three finals is one of football's great tragedies. Van Dijk, at 35, is almost certainly playing in his final World Cup. The last of the Oranje generation that reached the 2010 final's spiritual successors want to finally complete the mission. Will the Dutch ever lift the trophy?

Their 2022 victory over France (albeit a weakened side) showed what they are capable of. Breaking this barrier would be a seismic moment for Tunisian football.

interesting_fact: Belgium's golden generation is making its last stand. De Bruyne at 35, Lukaku at 33 - this is almost certainly their final World Cup together. They were ranked #1 in the world for years but never won a major trophy. The 2018 semifinal loss to France, the 2022 group-stage disaster, and the Euro 2024 quarterfinal exit haunt them. This is the last chance for redemption, and everyone knows the window has nearly closed.
prediction_notes: Belgium remain talented but are past their peak. De Bruyne's fitness is the single biggest variable - a fit De Bruyne and Belgium are contenders; without him they are vulnerable. They should advance from Group G but a deep run depends entirely on key players staying healthy.

style: Organized, physical, and direct. Cape Verde plays a disciplined, hard-working style that maximizes limited individual quality through teamwork. They use their athleticism effectively and are dangerous from set pieces.

offense.explanation: Ryan Mendes and Garry Rodrigues provide attacking quality. The team creates chances through hard work and set pieces rather than intricate passing.

defense.explanation: Defensively disciplined and difficult to break down. Cape Verde's organization compensates for the quality gap against top teams.

form: Players are spread across leagues in Portugal, Greece, and the Middle East. Individual form varies but the team's collective spirit and qualifying momentum provide confidence heading into the tournament.
world_cup_history: This is Cape Verde's first-ever World Cup appearance. With a population of around 590,000, they are one of the smallest nations ever to qualify for a World Cup. Their rise in African football has been one of the continent's great stories.
interesting_fact: Cape Verde, a volcanic archipelago of 590,000 people off West Africa's coast, has produced a diaspora that is twice as large as its home population. Many of the squad's players were born in Portugal, France, or the Netherlands. Their World Cup qualification united the global Cape Verdean community in an outpouring of emotion. For a nation with almost no football infrastructure, this is a miracle of ambition and identity.
prediction_notes: Cape Verde are among the weakest teams in the tournament and will face an enormous challenge in Group H with Spain and Uruguay. Their aim will be to compete with dignity and perhaps steal a point from Saudi Arabia. Simply being there is already the greatest achievement in their sporting history.

name: Saudi Arabia
qualification: Qualified through AFC qualifying, securing their place in the Asian third round. Saudi Arabia used their home advantage and investment in football to qualify for their seventh World Cup.
style: Counter-attacking with technical midfield play. Saudi Arabia has improved tactically under Mancini, playing a more structured, European-influenced style. They are dangerous on the break and use their technical midfielders to control tempo.

offense.explanation: Salem Al-Dawsari remains the main creative and goal-scoring threat. The attack is decent but heavily reliant on a few key players.

defense.explanation: Improved under Mancini but still prone to defensive errors against quality opposition. The domestic league experience limits defensive readiness for World Cup intensity.

form: Salem Al-Dawsari continues to be the Saudi league's standout local talent. The influx of stars like Neymar to the Saudi Pro League has raised the level of domestic competition, which benefits the national team players. However, the lack of players in top European leagues remains a limitation.
world_cup_history: Saudi Arabia have appeared in 6 World Cups. Their greatest moment was the 1994 tournament where they reached the Round of 16, including Saeed Al-Owairan's incredible solo goal against Belgium. In 2022, they famously beat Argentina in the group stage in one of the biggest World Cup upsets ever.
interesting_fact: Saudi Arabia's stunning 2-1 victory over Messi's Argentina in the 2022 World Cup opener remains one of the greatest upsets in football history. The nation declared a public holiday. Now they arrive at 2026 with Mancini as coach and a Saudi Pro League that has attracted global stars. Their Vision 2030 sports investment makes football central to the kingdom's transformation.
prediction_notes: Saudi Arabia are underdogs but have proven they can produce historic upsets, as they did against Argentina in 2022. Group H with Spain and Uruguay is extremely tough. Their best hope is to compete aggressively and possibly spring another surprise. Reaching the knockout rounds would require repeating their 2022 heroics.

name: Uruguay
qualification: Qualified through CONMEBOL South American qualifying, finishing in the top positions of the Eliminatorias. Uruguay's qualification was never in doubt as Bielsa's intense style produced consistent results.
style: Intense pressing, aggressive, and relentless under Bielsa. Uruguay plays with extraordinary energy and commitment, pressing opponents into errors. Bielsa's influence has added tactical sophistication to Uruguay's traditional garra charrúa fighting spirit.

offense.explanation: Darwin Núñez is one of the world's most dynamic strikers, supported by quality across the front line. Uruguay's attack is powerful, fast, and unpredictable.

defense.explanation: Ronald Araujo and José María Giménez form one of the best center-back partnerships in international football. The defense is physical, aggressive, and rarely gives up easy goals.

form: Valverde has been magnificent at Real Madrid, establishing himself as one of the world's best midfielders. Núñez has been prolific at Liverpool. Araujo is Barcelona's defensive rock. The squad is stacked with players at elite European clubs in top form.
world_cup_history: Uruguay won the first-ever World Cup in 1930 and won again in 1950 with the famous 'Maracanazo' against Brazil. They finished fourth in 2010 and reached the quarterfinals in 2018. A two-time champion with a rich World Cup heritage.
interesting_fact: Marcelo Bielsa's appointment transformed Uruguay from a good team to a great one. El Loco's obsessive preparation and tactical innovation have turned Uruguay into one of the most exciting teams in world football. With Valverde, Núñez, and Araujo all in their prime, this could be Uruguay's best squad since the Forlán-Suárez-Cavani era. The spirit of garra charrúa - Uruguay's famous never-say-die attitude - burns stronger than ever.
prediction_notes: Uruguay are genuine contenders who can beat anyone on their day. Bielsa's tactical genius combined with elite players at top clubs makes them dangerous. They should advance from Group H and could reach the semifinals. The question is whether Bielsa's intense style can be sustained over seven matches.

name: France
qualification: Qualified through UEFA qualifying, topping their group as reigning World Cup runners-up. France's qualification was straightforward, with their squad depth making them virtually unbeatable in qualifying.
style: Pragmatic and devastatingly effective in transition. France under Deschamps prioritizes defensive solidity and uses their incredible individual talent in attack to win games. They can absorb pressure and strike lethal counter-attacks with pace and power.

offense.explanation: Kylian Mbappé is the best player in the world, supported by a staggering array of attacking talent. France's offensive quality is unmatched - they have two full starting XIs worth of world-class attackers.

defense.explanation: Defensively excellent with Upamecano, Saliba, and Tchouaméni providing a strong spine. Deschamps always builds from a solid defensive foundation.

form: Mbappé has been exceptional in his first seasons at Real Madrid, justifying his status as the world's best player. Saliba has been Arsenal's defensive cornerstone. The depth is absurd - even players who can't make the squad would start for most other nations.
world_cup_history: France have won 2 World Cups (1998 as hosts, 2018 in Russia) and were runners-up in 2022 and 2006. They are the reigning runners-up after the epic 2022 final against Argentina. Historically one of the tournament's most successful nations.
interesting_fact: France lost the 2022 World Cup final in the most dramatic fashion imaginable - Mbappé scored a hat trick but they still lost to Argentina on penalties. The pain of that defeat has fueled French motivation. Deschamps, who coached France to the 2018 title and 2022 final, may be managing his last tournament. This could also be Griezmann's farewell. The hunger for redemption after that Qatar heartbreak makes France the most motivated team in the tournament.
prediction_notes: France are the favorites or co-favorites to win the tournament. Mbappé alone makes them dangerous, but the squad depth is staggering. The only question is whether Deschamps' pragmatic style can overcome the more expansive play of Spain or Germany. If Mbappé is fully fit, France are the team everyone wants to avoid.

name: Senegal
qualification: Qualified through CAF qualifying as one of Africa's strongest teams. Senegal topped their qualifying group, continuing their status as the leading African football nation.
style: Athletic, fast, and physically imposing. Senegal plays a direct, power-based style that uses pace on the wings and physicality in midfield. They press well and can overwhelm opponents with their energy and intensity.

offense.explanation: Ismaïla Sarr and the attacking corps provide pace and directness. Since Sadio Mané's peak years, the attack has evolved to be more of a collective threat.

defense.explanation: Kalidou Koulibaly anchors a strong defense. Senegal are difficult to break down and their physical presence makes them formidable in aerial duels and set-piece defense.

form: Nicolas Jackson has emerged as Chelsea's first-choice striker, adding much-needed goal-scoring to the squad. Sarr has been effective at Crystal Palace. The squad has a good base of Premier League experience.
world_cup_history: Senegal's first World Cup in 2002 was magical - they beat France in the opening match and reached the quarterfinals. They returned in 2018 (eliminated on fair play tiebreaker) and 2022 (Round of 16). They are Africa's most successful recent World Cup nation.
interesting_fact: Senegal's 2002 World Cup run, when they beat defending champions France and reached the quarterfinals as debutants, remains one of football's greatest fairy tales. Aliou Cissé, who captained that team and missed the crucial penalty in the quarterfinal, is now the coach seeking redemption. He has been managing Senegal since 2015, building them into Africa's #1 team and winning the 2021 AFCON. His journey from penalty heartbreak to coaching redemption is extraordinary.
prediction_notes: Senegal are strong dark horses with Premier League quality throughout the squad. They should compete for a knockout-round spot in Group I. Their physicality and pace can trouble any opponent, though they may struggle against the very best tactical setups.

name: Iraq
qualification: Qualified through AFC qualifying in a remarkable campaign. Iraq secured one of Asia's World Cup berths, returning to the tournament for the first time since 1986 in what was celebrated as a national triumph.
style: Tactical discipline with creative flair. Iraq plays organized football with an emphasis on defensive shape and quick transitions. The team has technically gifted players who can produce moments of individual brilliance.

offense.explanation: Ali Jasim and Mohanad Ali provide creative spark. The attack relies on individual moments of quality rather than sustained pressure.

defense.explanation: Well-organized under Spanish coaching influence. Iraq defend as a compact unit and are difficult to break down, though they lack the individual quality to match top nations.

form: Most players compete in the Iraqi or other Middle Eastern leagues, limiting exposure to the highest levels of competition. However, the team's collective spirit and qualifying momentum provide confidence.
world_cup_history: Iraq have appeared in one World Cup (1986 in Mexico), where they lost all three group games. Their qualification for 2026, 40 years later, was an emotional moment for a nation that has endured war, sanctions, and sectarian violence.
interesting_fact: Iraq's World Cup qualification is perhaps the most emotional story of the 2026 tournament. A nation that has endured decades of war, the ISIS occupation, sectarian violence, and massive displacement has found unity through football. When they qualified, celebrations erupted across Baghdad and Iraqi communities worldwide. For millions of Iraqis, this team represents hope that their country can achieve something beautiful amid so much pain. The 40-year wait since their last World Cup makes this even more poignant.
prediction_notes: Iraq are underdogs in Group I with France and Senegal, but their emotional motivation and tactical organization should not be underestimated. Picking up a point would be a massive achievement. They will look to be competitive in every match and enjoy the occasion.

name: Norway
qualification: Qualified through UEFA qualifying, securing their first World Cup berth since 1998. Norway's qualification was driven by Erling Haaland's incredible goal-scoring record in the qualifiers.
style: Direct and built around Haaland. Norway plays a pragmatic style that focuses on getting the ball to Erling Haaland as efficiently as possible. They press well and have improved tactically, but the system is designed to maximize their star striker's impact.

offense.explanation: Erling Haaland is arguably the best striker in the world. With Martin Ødegaard providing creativity, Norway's attacking axis is world-class. The challenge is the supporting cast.

defense.explanation: Adequate but not elite. The defense has improved under Solbakken but remains the weakest area of the team compared to top nations.

form: Haaland has continued to score at an otherworldly rate at Manchester City. Ødegaard has been Arsenal's captain and creative force, one of the Premier League's best players. Sørloth has been prolific at Atlético Madrid. The star players are in incredible form.
world_cup_history: Norway have appeared in 3 World Cups (1938, 1994, 1998) with a best result of Round of 16 in 1998. They famously beat Brazil 2-1 in the 1998 group stage. This is their first World Cup since then - a 28-year gap.
interesting_fact: Norway's return to the World Cup after 28 years is built around one man: Erling Haaland. The most prolific striker of his generation has single-handedly dragged Norway to the tournament. Combined with Ødegaard's creativity, Norway possesses one of the best attacking partnerships in football. The narrative is irresistible - can two players from a country of 5.5 million people carry their nation to World Cup glory?
prediction_notes: Norway are fascinating dark horses. Haaland and Ødegaard give them two genuinely world-class players, which is more than most teams can claim. They should advance from Group I, though France will be tough to overcome for the top spot. If Haaland catches fire, Norway could go on a memorable run.

name: Argentina
qualification: Qualified through CONMEBOL South American qualifying, topping the Eliminatorias as defending World Cup champions. Argentina's qualification was dominant, continuing their incredible run of success.
style: Balanced, intelligent football built around Messi's genius and strong defensive structure. Argentina under Scaloni plays fluid, attractive football that adapts to opponents. They can control possession or hit on the counter with devastating effect.

offense.explanation: Lionel Messi, even at 38, remains capable of magic. Julián Álvarez and Lautaro Martínez provide world-class striking options. The attack is versatile and clinical.

defense.explanation: Cristian Romero and Lisandro Martínez form an aggressive, high-quality center-back partnership. Emiliano Martínez is one of the world's best goalkeepers. Defensively excellent.

form: Álvarez has been excellent at Atlético Madrid. Lautaro has continued scoring at a high rate at Inter Milan. Emiliano Martínez remains world-class. Messi at Inter Miami is the question mark - his form in MLS is good but the competition level raises questions about his World Cup readiness.
world_cup_history: Argentina have won 3 World Cups (1978, 1986, 2022) and been runners-up 3 times. They are the defending champions after their epic 2022 triumph in Qatar, where Messi finally won the trophy in what was widely considered the greatest World Cup final ever.
interesting_fact: This is almost certainly Lionel Messi's final World Cup. The greatest player of all time, now 38, won the trophy in 2022 in the most dramatic final in history. Can he defend it? The entire football world will be watching to see if Messi can produce one more miracle. His farewell tour adds a layer of emotion to every Argentina match. Whether Messi starts or comes off the bench, his mere presence elevates everything around him.
prediction_notes: Argentina are defending champions and among the favorites. The squad is deeper than in 2022, with Álvarez and Lautaro providing insurance if Messi's body can't sustain a full tournament. The key variable is Messi's fitness and form. Even without peak Messi, this squad is good enough to reach the semifinals.

name: Algeria
qualification: Qualified through CAF qualifying. Algeria topped their African qualifying group, rebuilding after their disappointing 2022 AFCON defense where they were eliminated in the group stage.
style: Technical, possession-oriented football with North African flair. Algeria plays a controlled style that emphasizes ball retention and quick combinations. They have technically gifted players, many with dual French-Algerian nationality who grew up in European academies.

offense.explanation: Riyad Mahrez provides star quality and creativity. The supporting cast includes talented young players, but the attack lacks a consistent goal-scoring threat.

defense.explanation: Organized and competitive. The defense is solid against most opposition but can be exposed by elite attacking talent.

form: Bennacer has been important at AC Milan when fit. Gouiri has been effective in Ligue 1. Mahrez has adapted to life in the Saudi league. The squad has a blend of experienced campaigners and hungry young talent.
world_cup_history: Algeria have appeared in 4 World Cups (1982, 1986, 2010, 2014). They reached the Round of 16 in 2014 in Brazil, pushing Germany to extra time before losing 2-1. Their 1982 campaign was marked by the 'Disgrace of Gijón' scandal.
interesting_fact: Algeria's 1982 World Cup campaign remains one of football's greatest injustices - they beat West Germany 2-1 but were eliminated after Germany and Austria played out a contrived result known as the 'Disgrace of Gijón.' Algerian football has always carried this sense of injustice, and every World Cup is a chance to prove they belong at the highest level. Their 2019 AFCON title, with Mahrez's legendary free-kick goal, showed what they can achieve.
prediction_notes: Algeria are a solid Group J contender who could compete for second place behind Argentina. Their technical quality and AFCON experience make them competitive. Advancing to the knockout rounds would be a satisfying achievement.

name: Austria
qualification: Qualified through UEFA qualifying with an impressive campaign under Rangnick. Austria's pressing-based style earned them qualification, continuing the transformation that made them one of Euro 2024's best teams.
style: Gegenpressing at its finest. Rangnick, the godfather of modern pressing football, has turned Austria into one of the most intense teams in European football. They press relentlessly, win the ball high, and attack quickly in transition. It's exhausting to play against.

offense.explanation: The pressing system creates numerous chances through turnovers in dangerous areas. Individual quality from Laimer and Sabitzer supports the system. They score as a team rather than relying on one star.

defense.explanation: The best defense is pressing - Austria defend by not letting opponents have the ball. When they do defend traditionally, they are well-organized and compact.

form: Alaba's fitness after his long-term injury is the key question. Sabitzer has been excellent at Dortmund. Laimer is a regular at Bayern. Baumgartner has thrived in the Bundesliga. The squad is well-suited to Rangnick's demanding system.
world_cup_history: Austria have appeared in 7 World Cups with a best finish of third place in 1954. They also reached the second round in 1978 and 1982. However, they haven't appeared at a World Cup since 1998.
interesting_fact: Ralf Rangnick's Austria is the neutrals' favorite team. The father of gegenpressing, the man who inspired Klopp, Tuchel, and Nagelsmann, has created a system so intense that even top teams hate playing against them. Their Euro 2024 campaign, where they topped a group with France, showed what they're capable of. If Alaba returns from his serious knee injury, Austria become genuine dark-horse contenders.
prediction_notes: Austria are one of the most dangerous dark horses in the tournament. Rangnick's system makes them greater than the sum of their parts. They will be nightmarish to play against with their relentless pressing. The key questions are Alaba's fitness and whether they can sustain intensity over multiple knockout games.

name: Jordan
qualification: Qualified through AFC qualifying following their historic run to the 2023 Asian Cup final. Jordan used the momentum of that breakthrough to secure their first-ever World Cup berth.
style: Defensive solidity with quick counter-attacks. Jordan plays a disciplined, organized style that frustrates opponents. They defend deep in numbers and use pace on the break to create scoring opportunities.

offense.explanation: Limited attacking options but capable of scoring on the counter. The team creates fewer chances than most but can be clinical when opportunities arise.

defense.explanation: The defensive structure is Jordan's greatest strength. They are extremely difficult to break down and have built their success on not conceding.

form: Al-Tamari has been performing well in Ligue 1. Most other players compete in the Jordanian or other Middle Eastern leagues. The squad's collective spirit and defensive organization compensate for limited individual quality.
world_cup_history: This is Jordan's first-ever World Cup appearance. They reached the 2023 Asian Cup final, losing to Qatar, which was a breakthrough moment for Jordanian football and set the stage for World Cup qualification.
interesting_fact: Jordan's World Cup qualification is a story of an entire nation united. A small country surrounded by conflict - Iraq, Syria, Palestine - has found joy and unity through football. The 2023 Asian Cup final appearance shocked the football world, and World Cup qualification was the next miracle. King Abdullah II has personally championed the team, and the entire nation has rallied behind this squad. For Jordan, football is a rare source of national celebration in a troubled region.
prediction_notes: Jordan are heavy underdogs in Group J with Argentina, Algeria, and Austria. They will aim to be competitive and enjoy the experience. A point or a goal would be celebrated enormously. Their defensive discipline could make them a tough opponent for anyone on their day.

name: Portugal

offense.explanation: Rafael Leão, Bernardo Silva, and a deep pool of attackers make Portugal one of the most dangerous offensive teams in the world. Their ability to score from any situation is remarkable.

defense.explanation: Rúben Dias and the defensive unit are solid and experienced. The defense has improved under Martínez, though it can still be exposed on the counter.

form: Rafael Leão has been exceptional at Milan, establishing himself as one of the best wingers in world football. Bernardo Silva continues to be extraordinary at City. Bruno Fernandes has been United's most important player. The squad has immense quality.

interesting_fact: Portugal enters the 2026 World Cup in the shadow of tragedy. Diogo Jota's death in a car accident earlier in the year devastated the squad and the entire nation. The team has vowed to honor his memory, and his jersey number has been retired. Every match will carry immense emotional weight as Portugal plays for their fallen teammate. The squad is united by grief and determination to make Jota proud.
prediction_notes: Portugal are among the favorites with extraordinary attacking depth. The emotional motivation of honoring Diogo Jota could either inspire them to glory or become an emotional burden. On talent alone, they have the squad to reach the final. Their group should be navigated comfortably.

name: DR Congo
qualification: Qualified through CAF qualifying following their breakthrough 2024 AFCON quarterfinal run. DR Congo used the momentum of their continental success to qualify for only their second World Cup.
style: Athletic, powerful, and direct. DR Congo plays with incredible physicality and pace, using their athletic advantages to overpower opponents. The team combines raw talent with improving tactical discipline.

offense.explanation: Cédric Bakambu and the attacking options provide pace and power. The attack is dangerous in transition but can lack composure in the final third.

defense.explanation: Physical and imposing, but can be disorganized. The defense relies more on individual athleticism than collective structure.

form: Yoane Wissa has been excellent at Brentford, establishing himself as a reliable Premier League scorer. Mbemba brings experience from Marseille. The squad has a growing number of players at European clubs.
world_cup_history: DR Congo (then Zaire) appeared in the 1974 World Cup, where they lost all three group games including a 9-0 defeat to Yugoslavia. The infamous moment of Mwepu Ilunga kicking the ball away from a Brazilian free kick remains one of World Cup football's most memorable images. This is their first return in 52 years.
interesting_fact: DR Congo returns to the World Cup for the first time since 1974, when as Zaire they were humiliated 9-0 by Yugoslavia. The country has endured civil wars, dictatorships, and ongoing conflict in the decades since. Football is the one thing that unites this vast, diverse nation of 100 million people. Their qualification was celebrated across the country and in the massive Congolese diaspora. This is about far more than football - it's about a nation's pride and identity.
prediction_notes: DR Congo are exciting underdogs with raw athletic talent. In Group K with Portugal and Colombia, advancing will be very difficult. However, their physicality and pace can cause problems for anyone. A single victory would be a massive achievement for Congolese football.

name: Uzbekistan

style: Technical and tactical with a focus on ball possession. Uzbekistan plays organized, disciplined football influenced by their experienced European coaches. They combine Central Asian technical skill with defensive structure.

defense.explanation: Well-organized and disciplined. The defense is the foundation of their play, keeping games tight and competitive.

form: Shomurodov has gained valuable Serie A experience at Roma. Fayzullaev has been impressive in the Russian Premier League. The squad has a mix of European-based and domestic league players.
world_cup_history: This is Uzbekistan's first-ever World Cup appearance. They came agonizingly close in 2014 and 2018 qualifying but fell short in the final rounds each time. Qualifying for 2026 was a moment of national celebration.
interesting_fact: Uzbekistan have been the 'nearly men' of Asian football for decades, heartbreakingly falling at the final hurdle in multiple World Cup qualifying campaigns. Their qualification for 2026 was met with euphoric celebrations across the country. As a former Soviet republic that has quietly developed its football culture, this World Cup represents the culmination of decades of investment and dreams. They carry the hopes of Central Asian football.
prediction_notes: Uzbekistan are underdogs in Group K but will be well-organized and difficult to beat. Their first World Cup is about making a statement for Central Asian football. A point or a competitive showing would be a success. Their discipline could frustrate bigger teams.

qualification: Qualified through CONMEBOL South American qualifying. Colombia's impressive form under Lorenzo saw them finish strongly in the Eliminatorias, continuing their resurgence after reaching the 2024 Copa América final.
style: Exciting, attacking football with creative midfield play. Colombia plays a vibrant, entertaining style that emphasizes flair, creativity, and goals. They are one of the most enjoyable teams to watch, with quick passing and movement.

offense.explanation: Luis Díaz, Jhon Arias, and Rafael Santos Borré lead a dynamic attack. Colombia's offensive talent is deep and varied, with pace, creativity, and goals from multiple positions.

defense.explanation: Improved under Lorenzo but still the weaker side of the team. The emphasis on attacking football can leave defensive gaps.

James Rodríguez experienced a late-career renaissance at the 2024 Copa América and provides veteran creativity. The squad is brimming with confidence.
world_cup_history: Colombia have appeared in 6 World Cups with a best finish of quarterfinals in 2014. They reached the Round of 16 in 1990 and 2018. Their most famous World Cup moment is Carlos Valderrama's creative genius in the 1990s.
interesting_fact: Colombia's 2024 Copa América campaign, where they reached the final unbeaten before losing to Argentina, reignited Colombian football passion. James Rodríguez, written off by many, turned back the clock with vintage performances. Luis Díaz's father was kidnapped and later rescued during qualifying, a harrowing ordeal that demonstrated the personal stakes Colombian players face. This team plays with the weight of a nation on their shoulders and the joy of Colombian culture in their hearts.
prediction_notes: Colombia are genuine contenders with a deep, talented squad and excellent recent form. They should advance from Group K and have the attacking quality for a deep run. Their main weakness is defensive vulnerability - if they can tighten up at the back, they could reach the semifinals.

name: England
qualification: Qualified through UEFA qualifying under interim management before Tuchel took charge. England's qualification was straightforward, with their attacking talent overpowering opposition in the group.
style: Possession with attacking intent under Tuchel. England have evolved from Southgate's pragmatic approach to a more proactive, front-foot style. Tuchel demands high pressing, quick passing combinations, and aggressive wing play.

offense.explanation: Jude Bellingham, Bukayo Saka, and Phil Foden form a world-class attacking trio. Harry Kane provides elite finishing. England's attacking depth is extraordinary.

defense.explanation: The defense has been reorganized under Tuchel with a more aggressive pressing approach. Individual quality is high with stones and others, but the system is still being bedded in.

form: Bellingham has been sensational at Real Madrid, a Ballon d'Or contender. Saka has been Arsenal's talisman. Kane continues to score at a remarkable rate at Bayern Munich. Foden has cemented his status as one of the world's best. The squad is arguably the most talented England have ever assembled.
world_cup_history: England won the World Cup in 1966 as hosts. They reached the semifinals in 1990 and 2018, and the quarterfinals in 2002 and 2006. Under Southgate, they also reached the Euro 2020 and 2024 finals, losing both. The 'it's coming home' narrative endures.
interesting_fact: England's eternal heartbreak continues - they lost two consecutive European Championship finals under Southgate and have not won a major trophy since 1966. Tuchel was hired specifically to end the 60-year drought. The appointment of a German coach was controversial, but the FA prioritized winning over nationality. With the most talented squad in a generation, the pressure on England is immense. Bellingham, Saka, and Foden are determined not to become another 'nearly' generation.
prediction_notes: England are among the top favorites with a squad that rivals anyone in the tournament. Tuchel's tactical acumen combined with world-class players makes them genuine contenders. The mental barrier of winning a knockout tournament remains the biggest question. If they can overcome the psychological hurdle, they have the talent to win it all.

name: Croatia
qualification: Qualified through UEFA qualifying with a typically efficient campaign. Croatia's tournament pedigree and qualifying know-how secured their spot despite an aging core.
style: Midfield-dominated, possession-based football. Croatia's identity is built around their exceptional midfield. They control games through superior passing, positional play, and the intelligence of their central players. They can be patient in possession and lethal in transition.

offense.explanation: The midfield creativity of Modrić and successors creates chances, and the forwards are capable finishers. Not the most prolific but efficient and clinical.

defense.explanation: Experienced and well-organized. Joško Gvardiol has emerged as a world-class center-back. The defense is the foundation that allows the midfield to control games.

form: Modrić, at 40, continues to defy age at Real Madrid. Gvardiol has been outstanding at Manchester City. Kovačić provides Premier League quality in midfield. The squad blends veteran experience with emerging talent, though the transition from the Modrić generation is ongoing.
world_cup_history: Croatia were runners-up in 2018 (losing to France in the final) and finished third in 2022. They also finished third in their debut World Cup in 1998. For a nation of 4 million people, Croatia's World Cup record is extraordinary.
interesting_fact: Luka Modrić at 40 years old is attempting something almost unimaginable - one final World Cup. The 2018 Ballon d'Or winner and greatest Croatian footballer ever has delayed retirement specifically for this tournament. His last dance in a Croatia shirt is one of the most emotional storylines in the entire World Cup. A nation of 4 million that reached two consecutive World Cup finals/semifinals will rally around their aging maestro one last time.
prediction_notes: Croatia remain dangerous despite an aging core. Modrić's brilliance, Gvardiol's defensive excellence, and Dalić's tournament expertise make them genuine contenders to advance. The question is whether the legs can hold up. If Modrić produces one more magical tournament, Croatia could surprise again.

name: Ghana
qualification: Qualified through CAF qualifying. Ghana navigated their African qualifying group to secure their spot at the World Cup, continuing their tradition of African football excellence.
style: Athletic, fast, and physically imposing. Ghana plays with tremendous energy and pace, using their athletic advantages to compete with technically superior teams. They are direct in attack and aggressive in defense.

offense.explanation: Inaki Williams and other attackers provide pace and directness. The attack is fast and powerful but can lack composure in the final third against organized defenses.

defense.explanation: The defense has been inconsistent. Individual talent exists but organizational issues can lead to costly errors against top-quality opposition.

form: Mohammed Kudus has been brilliant at West Ham, becoming one of the Premier League's most exciting players. Thomas Partey remains important at Arsenal. The 'new wave' of Ghana internationals - players who chose Ghana over European birth nations - has elevated the squad's quality.
world_cup_history: Ghana have appeared in 4 World Cups (2006, 2010, 2014, 2022). Their greatest moment was reaching the quarterfinals in 2010, where Asamoah Gyan's penalty miss against Uruguay (after Suárez's deliberate handball) broke the hearts of an entire continent.
interesting_fact: Ghana's 2010 quarterfinal heartbreak against Uruguay - Luis Suárez's handball on the line, Asamoah Gyan's missed penalty - remains the most emotionally devastating moment in African World Cup history. Africa's dream of a first semifinal died that night. Many of the current squad, including dual-nationals like Iñaki Williams and Tariq Lamptey, chose Ghana specifically to help the nation overcome that trauma and achieve what the 2010 team could not.
prediction_notes: Ghana are competitive in Group L with England and Croatia but face a tough path to the knockout rounds. Their athletic talent and Premier League experience give them a chance to cause upsets. Kudus is the X-factor who could elevate the team beyond expectations.

name: Panama
qualification: Qualified through CONCACAF qualifying. Panama earned one of the confederation's expanded World Cup berths, continuing their remarkable rise in Central American football.
style: Physical, disciplined, and counter-attacking. Panama plays a hard-working, organized style that relies on defensive solidity and quick breaks. They are aggressive in challenges and use set pieces effectively.

offense.explanation: Limited in sustained attacking play but capable of scoring on the break and from set pieces. The team is efficient rather than creative.

defense.explanation: The team's foundation. Panama defend with numbers, commitment, and organization. They make it very difficult for opponents to create clear chances.

form: Most players compete in Central American leagues or MLS. The squad lacks European top-flight representation but makes up for it with collective spirit and organizational discipline under Christiansen.
world_cup_history: Panama appeared in their first World Cup in 2018 in Russia, where they lost all three group games but scored their first-ever World Cup goal (against England). The qualification itself was celebrated as the greatest moment in Panamanian sporting history.
interesting_fact: When Panama qualified for the 2018 World Cup, the president declared a national holiday and grown men wept in the streets. The entire country came to a standstill. Now at their second World Cup, Panama is no longer just happy to be there - they want results. For a tiny Central American nation wedged between CONCACAF giants, every World Cup appearance is a triumph of determination over resources.
prediction_notes: Panama are underdogs in Group L with England and Croatia but will fight for every inch. Their physical, combative style can make them uncomfortable opponents. Repeating their 2018 experience of simply competing at the World Cup is the baseline; a point would be a genuine achievement.
